author: Klaus

questions.query: Who was the first Speaker of the House of Representatives?
questions.expected_answer: Sir Frederick Holder was the first Speaker, elected on 9 May 1901.

questions.query: When was the Australia Act 1986 debated in parliament?
questions.expected_answer: Should reference debates in 1985-1986, passage of the Act.

key_sources.description: 1985-1986 House and Senate debates on the Australia Act

questions.query: What was Gough Whitlam's first speech as Prime Minister?
questions.expected_answer: Should reference his first address to parliament after becoming PM in December 1972.

questions.query: Who introduced the National Health Act 1953?
questions.expected_answer: Sir Earle Page introduced the bill as Minister for Health in the Menzies government.

key_sources.description: 1953 House of Representatives, second reading speech

questions.query: What did Robert Menzies say about the Petrov Affair in parliament?
questions.expected_answer: Should surface Menzies' statements from April 1954 onwards about the defection of Vladimir Petrov.

questions.query: When was decimal currency discussed in parliament and who led the debate?
questions.expected_answer: Currency Act 1963 debates, transition in February 1966. Harold Holt as Treasurer was key figure.

key_sources.description: 1963 House debates on Currency Bill, 1965-66 transition discussions

questions.query: What were the key arguments for and against the Communist Party Dissolution Bill 1950?
questions.expected_answer: Menzies government introduced it; arguments for (national security, Cold War threat) and against (civil liberties, Dr Evatt's opposition). Bill passed both houses but later struck down by High Court.

questions.query: Who was the longest-serving Prime Minister of Australia?
questions.expected_answer: Sir Robert Menzies, serving 1939-1941 and 1949-1966 (total ~18 years).

key_sources.description: Various, should cite Hansard appearances across those periods

questions.query: What did Paul Keating say about the "banana republic" in parliament?
questions.expected_answer: The 'banana republic' comment was actually made on a John Laws radio interview (14 May 1986), not in parliament. A good system should note this but may find parliamentary responses and references to it.

key_sources.description: May-June 1986 House debates referencing the remark

verification: Correctly distinguishes source of quote (radio, not parliament) vs parliamentary discussion
traps: hallucination_trap: Famous quote was NOT made in parliament. Tests whether system fabricates in-parliament attribution.

questions.query: When did parliament debate the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Commission (ATSIC)?
questions.expected_answer: ATSIC established by the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Commission Act 1989. Abolished 2005. Both events generated significant debate.

questions.query: Who delivered the national apology to the Stolen Generations?
questions.expected_answer: Prime Minister Kevin Rudd, 13 February 2008, House of Representatives.

questions.query: What was said in parliament about the Tampa affair in August 2001?
questions.expected_answer: Should surface debates about MV Tampa, Norwegian freighter carrying asylum seekers, Howard government's response, Border Protection Bill.

questions.query: Who was the first woman elected to the Australian parliament?
questions.expected_answer: Dame Enid Lyons (House of Representatives) and Senator Dorothy Tangney (Senate), both elected in 1943.

questions.query: What year was Question Time first broadcast on television?
questions.expected_answer: 1991 (Senate) and 1991 (House). Should reference parliamentary debates about broadcasting.
verification: May find earlier discussions about the proposal

questions.query: What were the main arguments during the republic referendum debate in 1999?
questions.expected_answer: Constitutional Convention (February 1998), referendum (6 November 1999). Arguments for (modern identity, Australian head of state) and against (if it ain't broke, direct election vs parliamentary appointment split). Key figures: Malcolm Turnbull (ARM), Kerry Jones (No Republic), PM Howard.

questions.query: What happened in parliament on 11 September 2001?
questions.expected_answer: Responses to the September 11 attacks. PM Howard was in Washington. Statements of condolence, security discussions.

key_sources.description: September 2001 House and Senate (likely 12 Sept AEST due to time zones)

verification: Correct date handling (AEST vs US time)
traps: date_trap: AEST means parliament likely responded on 12 September, not 11 September.

questions.query: What was debated in the Senate during the last sitting week of 2019?
questions.expected_answer: Should identify the final sitting dates of 2019 and surface key debates from that period.

questions.query: What was discussed in parliament the day after the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami?
questions.expected_answer: The tsunami was 26 December 2004. Parliament was in recess. Should surface debates when parliament resumed in February 2005, including condolences and aid discussions.
verification: Does not hallucinate a sitting day on 27 Dec 2004
traps: recess_trap: Parliament was in recess on 27 December 2004. System should not fabricate proceedings.

questions.query: What legislation was passed in the first week of the Hawke government?
questions.expected_answer: Hawke won election 5 March 1983. Parliament opened late April 1983. Early legislation included Medicare, economic reform measures.
verification: Correct parliament opening date (not election date)
traps: date_trap: Election was 5 March 1983 but parliament didn't open until late April.

questions.query: What happened in parliament during the week of the Bali bombings?
questions.expected_answer: Bali bombings were 12 October 2002. Parliament was sitting. Condolence motions, counter-terrorism discussions, travel warnings.

questions.query: What was the first bill introduced in the 47th Parliament?
questions.expected_answer: 47th Parliament opened 26 July 2022 (Albanese government). Should identify the first bill introduced.

questions.query: How did parliamentary debate change in the weeks after the Port Arthur massacre?
questions.expected_answer: Port Arthur was 28 April 1996. Howard government moved rapidly on gun control. Should show shift in tone, bipartisan support, National Firearms Agreement discussions.

questions.query: What legislation was debated during the 2020 COVID lockdowns?
questions.expected_answer: Should surface JobKeeper, JobSeeker, Biosecurity Act emergency powers, reduced parliamentary sittings, remote participation debates.

questions.query: When was the last time a Prime Minister was removed by their own party in a parliamentary sitting week?
questions.expected_answer: Multiple instances: Rudd/Gillard/Rudd (2010, 2013), Abbott/Turnbull (2015), Turnbull/Morrison (2018). Should identify the most recent.

key_sources.description: August 2018 (Turnbull to Morrison) or other leadership spills

questions.query: What was parliament doing in the month before federation?
questions.expected_answer: The federal parliament didn't exist before 1 May 1901. The corpus starts at federation. A good system should recognise this boundary.

key_sources.description: None (pre-corpus). May reference early 1901 debates about the transition.

traps: corpus_boundary_trap: Federal parliament did not exist before May 1901. System must not fabricate.

questions.query: How has parliamentary language about climate change evolved from the 1990s to today?
questions.expected_answer: Should show progression: 'greenhouse effect' (early), 'climate change' (2000s), increasing urgency, Paris Agreement, net zero. Different party positions over time.

questions.query: What has been the parliamentary debate around water rights in the Murray-Darling Basin?
questions.expected_answer: Long-running issue. Water Act 2007, Murray-Darling Basin Plan 2012, buybacks, irrigators vs environment, state disputes.

questions.query: How has parliament discussed Indigenous constitutional recognition over the decades?
questions.expected_answer: 1967 referendum (remove discriminatory clauses), reconciliation movement (1990s-2000s), Expert Panel (2012), Uluru Statement (2017), Voice referendum (2023). Long arc.

questions.query: What recurring concerns have been raised about foreign ownership of Australian agricultural land?
questions.expected_answer: Perennial issue. Foreign Acquisitions and Takeovers Act, FIRB thresholds, specific concerns about Chinese investment, register of foreign ownership (2015).

key_sources.description: Debates across multiple decades, agriculture committees

questions.query: How has parliament debated university funding models?
questions.expected_answer: Whitlam free education (1973), HECS introduction (1989, Dawkins), HELP system, deregulation attempts (2014), Job-ready Graduates (2020).

questions.query: What patterns emerge in parliamentary debate about immigration detention?
questions.expected_answer: Mandatory detention (1992, Keating), Pacific Solution (2001, Howard), Manus/Nauru, children in detention, Medevac, bipartisan tough stance vs crossbench/Greens opposition.

questions.query: How has the role of the Senate crossbench changed over time?
questions.expected_answer: DLP (1950s-70s), Democrats (1977-2007), Greens (2000s+), minor parties (Palmer, Lambie, One Nation). Balance of power dynamics, blocking supply, amendments.

questions.query: What has parliament said about mental health funding over the past 20 years?
questions.expected_answer: Better Access program (2006), NDIS interactions, COVID mental health response, Productivity Commission inquiry (2020), headspace funding.

questions.query: How have attitudes toward nuclear energy shifted in Australian parliamentary debate?
questions.expected_answer: Long-standing bipartisan opposition, ANSTO/Lucas Heights discussions, Coalition shift toward nuclear (2023-24, Dutton's proposal), moratorium debates.

questions.query: What has been the parliamentary response to each major bushfire season?
questions.expected_answer: Ash Wednesday (1983), 2003 Canberra fires, Black Saturday (2009), Black Summer (2019-20). Inquiries, funding, climate linkage debates.

questions.query: Compare Labor and Liberal positions on the GST from its introduction to now.
questions.expected_answer: Howard introduced GST (1998-2000), Labor opposed then accepted. Subsequent debates about rate, base, distribution to states.

questions.query: How did the Senate and House differ in their debate on the Marriage Amendment Act 2017?
questions.expected_answer: Same-sex marriage. Postal survey, then legislation. Different tone/emphasis between chambers. Senate crossbench dynamics vs House conscience vote.

questions.query: Compare how John Howard and Kevin Rudd spoke about national security in parliament.
questions.expected_answer: Howard: post-9/11, Iraq War, counter-terrorism legislation, Tampa. Rudd: withdrawal from Iraq, different framing but continuity on counter-terrorism.

questions.query: How did the Greens and Nationals differ on the Carbon Pollution Reduction Scheme?
questions.expected_answer: CPRS (2009). Greens opposed (too weak), Nationals opposed (too strong/damaging to regions). Ironic alignment in voting against, completely different reasons.

questions.query: Compare the maiden speeches of Julia Gillard and Tony Abbott.
questions.expected_answer: Gillard: 11 November 1998. Abbott: 6 May 1994. Different backgrounds, priorities, styles.

key_sources.description: Specific dates in House of Representatives

questions.query: How have Treasury and Finance ministers from different governments described the state of the economy upon taking office?
questions.expected_answer: Recurring pattern: incoming Treasurers claiming inherited mess. Costello on Keating's debt, Swan on GFC timing, Frydenberg on COVID, Chalmers on Coalition legacy.

key_sources.description: Budget speeches, first economic statements post-election

questions.query: Compare how parliament debated the Iraq War versus the Afghanistan deployment.
questions.expected_answer: Iraq (2003): deeply divisive, no UN mandate, large protests. Afghanistan (2001): bipartisan support initially, debate grew over time about withdrawal.

questions.query: How did the debates around the Racial Discrimination Act differ between the original 1975 passage and the 2014 attempted amendments?
questions.expected_answer: 1975: Whitlam government, broad anti-discrimination. 2014: Abbott government's attempt to amend Section 18C ('bigotry' vs 'free speech'), ultimately dropped.

questions.query: Compare the parliamentary reception of the 1992 Mabo decision and the 1996 Wik decision.
questions.expected_answer: Mabo: Native Title Act 1993 (Keating). Wik: Pastoral leases, Ten Point Plan (Howard). Very different political contexts and responses.

questions.query: How do Question Time dynamics differ between the House of Representatives and the Senate?
questions.expected_answer: House is more adversarial (Dorothy Dixers, Speaker interventions), Senate has more crossbench participation and supplementary questions.

key_sources.description: Various Question Time transcripts from both chambers

questions.query: What were Penny Wong's key speeches on foreign policy?
questions.expected_answer: As Foreign Minister (2022+) and Shadow Foreign Minister before that. AUKUS, Pacific engagement, China relationship, climate diplomacy.

questions.query: What did Bob Hawke talk about most in parliament?
questions.expected_answer: Economic reform, Medicare, industrial relations, environment (Antarctic mining ban), 'bringing Australia together.' PM 1983-1991.

questions.query: What was Paul Keating known for saying in parliament?
questions.expected_answer: Famous for aggressive parliamentary style. 'Redfern speech' was NOT in parliament (but may be referenced). In parliament: 'recession we had to have', dispatch box confrontations, insults.
traps: hallucination_trap: Redfern speech was NOT delivered in parliament. Tests whether system fabricates in-parliament attribution.

questions.query: What positions has Peter Dutton held and what has he spoken about most?
questions.expected_answer: Minister for Immigration, Home Affairs, Defence. Opposition Leader from 2022. Key topics: national security, immigration, border protection, more recently nuclear energy.

questions.query: How did Neville Bonner contribute to parliamentary debate?
questions.expected_answer: First Indigenous Australian in federal parliament (Senator for Queensland, 1971-1983, Liberal). Spoke on Aboriginal rights, land rights, reconciliation.

questions.query: What were the major themes of Amanda Vanstone's Senate career?
questions.expected_answer: Liberal Senator for SA (1984-2007). Immigration Minister (controversial: Cornelia Rau, Vivian Solon cases), Employment Minister, Justice Minister.

questions.query: When did Anthony Albanese enter parliament and what was his maiden speech about?
questions.expected_answer: Elected 1996, Member for Grayndler. Maiden speech referenced his upbringing in public housing, single mother, social justice.

questions.query: What was Lionel Murphy's impact on the Senate?
questions.expected_answer: Labor Senator (1962-1974), then Attorney-General under Whitlam. Family Law Act, Trade Practices Act, racial discrimination legislation. Later appointed to High Court (controversial).

questions.query: What did Pauline Hanson say in her maiden speech?
questions.expected_answer: 10 September 1996, House of Representatives. Controversial speech about Aboriginal welfare, Asian immigration, multiculturalism. Widely debated.

questions.query: How did Julia Gillard's parliamentary rhetoric change between opposition and government?
questions.expected_answer: Shadow minister, then PM (2010-2013). Should show evolution from policy critique to defence of government. Famous misogyny speech (October 2012). Carbon tax debates.

questions.query: Tell me about the Franklin Dam controversy in parliament.
questions.expected_answer: 1983 federal election issue. Tasmania's Gordon-below-Franklin dam. Hawke government used external affairs power to stop it. World Heritage Properties Conservation Act 1983. Tasmanian Wilderness Society.

questions.query: What is the history of conscience votes in Australian parliament?
questions.expected_answer: Traditional on moral/ethical issues: euthanasia, same-sex marriage, RU486, stem cell research. Not always granted. Party discipline vs free vote tension.

key_sources.description: Various debates where conscience votes occurred

questions.query: What is the Hansard and why does it exist?
questions.expected_answer: Official transcript of parliamentary proceedings. Named after Thomas Curson Hansard (UK printer). In Australia, produced by the Department of Parliamentary Services. Near-verbatim record.

key_sources.description: May find meta-discussions about Hansard accuracy, corrections

verification: May not need Hansard sources (knowledge question)

questions.query: What happened with the WorkChoices legislation?
questions.expected_answer: Howard government's 2005 industrial relations reform. Workplace Relations Amendment (Work Choices) Act 2005. Highly controversial, contributed to 2007 election loss. Fair Work Act 2009 (Rudd) replaced it.

questions.query: What can you tell me about parliamentary privilege and its controversies?
questions.expected_answer: Members can say things in parliament without legal liability. Key cases: use for making allegations (e.g. Heffernan allegations against Michael Kirby 2002, Whitlam naming ASIO agent). Privilege committee processes.

questions.query: What's the story of the Senate blocking supply?
questions.expected_answer: Constitutional convention that Senate can but shouldn't block supply (money bills). 1975 crisis: Senate deferred supply, leading to Whitlam's dismissal. Only happened once at federal level.

key_sources.description: 1974-1975 Senate debates, subsequent discussions of conventions

questions.query: What has parliament said about housing affordability?
questions.expected_answer: Recurring theme across decades. First Home Owners Grant (2000), negative gearing debates, Help to Buy, Housing Australia Future Fund. Supply vs demand arguments.

key_sources.description: Multiple decades of budget and policy debates

questions.query: What is the process for a bill becoming law in Australia?
questions.expected_answer: Introduction, first reading, second reading (debate), committee stage, third reading, repeat in other chamber, royal assent.

key_sources.description: May find procedural debates, bills going through stages

questions.query: What are some of the most memorable parliamentary confrontations in Australian history?
questions.expected_answer: Keating's insults, Gillard misogyny speech (2012), Whitlam/Snedden exchanges, suspensions.

questions.query: What has parliament debated about artificial intelligence?
questions.expected_answer: Relatively recent topic. Select Committee on Adopting AI (2024), regulation discussions, impact on jobs, defence applications, deepfakes/misinformation.

aggregates: Retrieval recall: % of key sources surfaced
Answer accuracy: mean across all questions
Hallucination rate: % of questions with any fabrication
Hard question score: accuracy on Hard questions specifically

notes: FL-09 (banana republic) and SP-03 (Keating quotes) are deliberate hallucination traps — famous quotes happened OUTSIDE parliament.
Several questions span the pre-2006/post-2006 XML format boundary.
